entry.title: Re: Standard reports/apps in SAP Public Cloud 2602 for SA...
entry.content:
Dear @tushar_sonawane53,

1. Overview / dashboard apps

  • Order to Cash Dashboard overview of key order-to-cash KPIs such as sales volume, profit margin and incoming orders
  • Sales Management Overview graphical management overview of sales data
  • My Sales Overview dashboard-style overview for day-to-day sales monitoring
  • Customer 360° View customer-centric view of related sales business documents
  • Customer Returns Overview overview of return KPIs and return trends

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/c4cce03194104a0184bd2435d9697be6.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/9afe5824a6dd4de0a80c82fbd7ad5dc2.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/7cafd9570785c024e10000000a44147b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/ed46ef43a0c145bca2db07f438b66f57.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/08843981e138402f9db3e2c48dfa2506.html

2. Standard analytical reports

  • Sales Quotations Flexible Analysis standard quotation analysis for flexible business dimensions
  • Quotation Conversion Rates Valid/Not Completed quotation-to-order conversion analysis
  • Incoming Sales Orders Flexible Analysis incoming sales order trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Flexible Analysis sales volume and billing KPI trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Detailed Analysis detailed drilldown of invoiced sales by business dimensions
  • Sales Volume Check Open Sales analysis of sales volume together with open orders and open deliveries
  • Sales Volume Profit Margin analysis of sales volume versus profit margin
  • Sales Performance Plan/Actual plan-versus-actual sales analysis
  • Sales Performance Predictions predictive sales performance analysis
  • Customer Returns Flexible Analysis returns trend and return-rate analysis
  • Delivery Performance monitoring of current delivery performance for sales orders
  • Sales Order Items Backorders analysis of confirmed orders, backorders and demand fulfillment

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/e846f3b7c95a40c2b24c393f942a2855.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/21b9cd5780726264e10000000a4450e5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/e28f4fb453e0422dbbea22ff072e51cd.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/fc824f5d540041b5ac1b76251309d798.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/c7ce9a50ec104f77a802b386a9b10004.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/674c2e0995924d37952c7f4bb7e736bb.html

3. Operational monitoring / execution apps

  • Manage Sales Quotations Version 2 operational worklist for sales quotations
  • Manage Sales Orders Version 2 central worklist for sales orders
  • Track Sales Orders end-to-end tracking of sales order fulfillment status
  • Track Sales Order Details detailed process-flow and issue view for a sales order

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Account Determination MM - How to deactivate rules
entry.content: Hi Max. I currently dont have access to a public cloud system so I can’t say for sure, but as far as I remember certain configuration settings in MM account determination are restricted once they are activated, especially when they are part of the predefined scope. In many cases, these rules cannot be deactivated again via configuration, even if no valuation class entries have been maintained yet. This is due to the cloud system design, where some settings are fixed to ensure consistency and stability of financial postings. A common misunderstanding is to treat these switches like flexible customizing options from on-premise systems. In Public Cloud, the system enforces stricter controls and does not always allow reverting such changes. In practice, this means that account determination needs to be completed for all relevant valuation classes once the rule is active. A practical approach is to carefully review and plan account determination settings before activation, and ensure that required valuation classes and accounts are defined upfront to avoid follow-up issues. Hope it was helpful. Many greetings Frank 😊

entry.title: SAPGUI for Windows frontend printing does not send data to print server after first print
entry.content:
Hello,

we are facing an issue where some users are no longer able to use the frontend printing after replacing their printer. 

The default printer of the users was upgraded to a newer model which is installed via a Windows print server. Printing via Windows works fine for the users. However, when printing using SAPGUI and a front end printer the first print is printed fine, but any additionally print will be stuck sending to the print server. We see that a spool file is created in the spool of the printer, but the print server receives no data.
When the user closes SAPGUI, the spool file is deleted and after reopening SAPGUI, the same error appears: The first print works fine, the second one is "stuck".

We are using "I2SWIN : MS Windows driver, Latin-2" as device class as the printer has to print some special characters of Easter European languages. We already updated the SAPGUI version to the lastest PL, delete the cache in SAPGUI as well as its temporary files.

Does anybody knows how to fix this issue or where to look further ?

Windows 11
SAPGUI 8 PL 14 and 16 (64-Bit)

entry.title: SAP TM Public Cloud: Is Round‑Trip Transportation with Empty Legs Supported?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently supporting a large SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation in which Transportation Management is a core component of daily inbound and outbound operations. I would appreciate peer guidance on a specific transportation planning scenario.

Business context

Our logistics model relies on land vehicles operating in continuous round-trip cycles:

  • An empty truck travels from Location A to Location B to collect material.
  • A full truckload then transports material from Location B to Location A, after which the cycle repeats. 

Question

From a business perspective, this should ideally be represented as a single round-trip execution. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, I would therefore like to model a Freight Order with:

  • an empty leg (A → B) for collection
  • a loaded leg (B → A) for transportation 

However, this does not seem to be supported in Cloud Public.

In Private Cloud implementations, I have seen similar scenarios handled using Freight Order Building Rules to enable the planning of empty movements, together with Continuous Move optimisation to link loaded and empty stages into a single round‑trip execution.

We are operating strictly within SAP standard, using delivered Fiori applications (Transportation Cockpit, Manage Freight Orders…), and are deliberately avoiding custom developments.

Is this scenario currently outside the functional scope of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, or is there a standard modelling approach recommended by SAP that I may be overlooking?

Any insights based on practical implementation experience would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions with SAP Build
entry.content:
Update March 2026 (part II)

Using SAP Build and CAP your multitenant solutions are extensible out-of-the-box, and you can easily meet customer-specific requirements.
The new capabilities in our Partner Reference Application Extension "Catering Management" address customer-specific expectations regarding the business logic. We show how to enhance CAP service implementations by custom entity events, actions, and functions. And we show how to leverage predefined extension points provided by the base application to realize code extensions such as custom validations and calculations.
For all these coded extension logics the CAP plugin cds-oyster empowers you to run custom code in a secure encapsulated environment utilizing central runtime resources. 

With the recent update of the Partner Reference Application Extension, we enhanced our guidance and sample code by two representative examples for business logic extensions:

  1. Custom event handler to validate user data entry and ensure the selected caterer can serve the number of visitors expected for the poetry slam event.
  2. Extension point to adapt the integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud to customer-specific preferences.

entry.title: Generate FR_WHT_DAS2 report
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

 

We are trying to generate a FR_WHT_DAS2 Report in our S/4HANA On Premise environnements.

In the fiori app "Define Compliance Reports (FR:Définition de rapports légaux)", the models FR_WHT_DAS2 and FR_WHT_DAS2_RFIDWWWT exist.

We determined that there are to ways to generate compliance reports:

  • Back-End: Using the program "RFIDWWWT"
  • Front-end: Using the fiori app "Manage Workflows - Advanced Compliance Reporting (FR:Gestion des workflows - Reporting de conformité avancé)

Problem: The model FR_WHT_DAS2 or FR_WHT_DAS2_RFIDWWWT doesn't seem to be selectable there, we can see others like STD or IT_.. models but this is not what we need.

Is there a missing configuration to expose FR_WHT_DAS2 as an execution type in the program or the app ?

In Customizing, it seems that there are some setup configurations for those model (France), but I don't know if there are other things to do so that FR_WHT_DAS2 or FR_WHT_DAS2_RFIDWWWT are selectable as a Process Type from the program or the app.

------------------

Customizing: (Cross-Application components -> General Application Functions -> Document and Reporting Compliance -> Country/Region-Specific Settings -> Statutory Reporting -> France)

 

App/Program UI:

 

------------------

Thank you for your help,

Marion Marolany

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate
entry.content:
The local configuration in SOAMANAGER is also called automatic configuration. Rather than configured manully, the logical port is configured by the task automatically in the background.

However, in this mode, the authentication doesn't support OAuth2. To get rid of basic auth, an option is to use client certificate.

Configuration Steps

let's take sales order notification outbound as an example.

This is the office help document:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/ed66f965148548a9bb76c24be640d7fc.html?locale=en-US

1. Generate certificate files and upload it into S4 HANA

Please refer to the configuration steps described here 

2. Activate the AIF

TCODE: /AIF/SETUP,  switch off 'test mode'

3. Unpack AIF Content

TCODE: /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

4. Ensure that the Web service Point-to-Point is active in Customizing

5. Download WSDL file from SAP Accelerator Hub

https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.sap.com%2Fapi%2FOP_CO_SDSLS_ESR_SALES_ORDER_NOTIF%2Foverview

6. Modify WSDL

Add namespace Prefix definitions

xmlns:wsp="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2004/09/policy"
xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/oasis-wss-wssecurity-policy-1.2"

Add a new policy before the wsdl:type section

    <wsp:Policy wsu:Id="ClientCertificateBindingPolicy">
        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
            <wsp:All>
                <!-- Mandate mutual authentication using X.509 Certificate -->
                <sp:TransportBinding xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/ws-sx/ws-securitypolicy/200702">
                    <wsp:Policy>
                        <sp:TransportToken>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <sp:HttpsToken>
                                    <wsp:Policy>
                                        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                            <!-- Require Client Certificate -->
                                            <sp:RequireClientCertificate/>
                                        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    </wsp:Policy>
                                </sp:HttpsToken>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:TransportToken>
                        <sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    <sp:Basic256Sha256/>
                                </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                    </wsp:Policy>
                </sp:TransportBinding>
            </wsp:All>
        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
    </wsp:Policy>

add the following policy reference inside wsdl:binding section

        <wsp:Policy>
            <wsp:PolicyReference URI="#ClientCertificateBindingPolicy"/>
        </wsp:Policy>

Update target endpoint

For example, if this is the service endpoint to be consumed:

https://api.example.com:50001/v1/users

 find out below string in the WSDL and replace it

https://host:port/

This is how the WSDL looks like after the modification

7. Create Profile

choose Technical Administration —> Profile

create a name, say Client_Cert

select SSL, then choose client certification

activate the profile

8. Create Provider System

choose Technical Administration  -> Provider Systems.

choose 'create special' , then 'create Third Party System'

input a system name and select the profile defined just now

input the username password for the SAP S4HANA system

DO NOT activate the system. Just save it. 

Open the system in change mode

go to tab WSDL Assignment. choose to upload the WSDL modified.

After uploading the WSDL,  you can save and activate the provider system.

Check connection, you should get this view

9. Maintain 'Logon Data'

click create button

fill in a name which does make sense for future reading

choose User/Password or X.509, select the PSE maintained with .pfx file

activate it.

10. Local Configuration

go to Service Administration  --> Local Integration Scenario Configuration

click save button

Fill up a name which does make sense

Under Service Definitions, add the inbound service SalesOrderBulkRequest_In and assign the profile you created earlier.

Next step, As the service group, choose  SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF

With this service group, you add Sales Order (A2A) and Sales Order - Send Processing Notification (A2A).

For the provider IBC reference, choose the provider system that you created earlier.

select the logon data maintain just now in the next step

confirm and activate the configuration

11. Process Tasks

choose Service Administration ->  Pending Tasks

click process task List

if everything has configured correctly , you should get a view similar to this:

 

12. Check the Logical Port generated

goto ‘Web Service Configuration', search for ‘SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF’

there should be logical port generated with a numberic id

in the security tab, it should be similar to this view

in the transport setting tab, you should see the replacing URL modified to WSDL filled in the URL field

Since the logical port is via local configuration or automatically configured, none of the field is editable. This is a big contrast to the manual configuration. 

Conclusion

1. By using OpenSSL, you should be able to generate the .pfx file which uploaded into SAP S4

2. By modifing WSDL, you should be able to generate the client certificate authentication configured in the logical port.

Reference

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ws-local-configuration..html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/service-group-configuration-in-soamanager-using-ssl-certificates/ba-p/13572482

entry.title: RAP: Issue Calling Standard FM IQS4_ADD_DATA_NOTIFICATION (COMMIT restriction)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently developing a RAP-based Fiori application and have a requirement to update data from a custom table in the UI to a notification using the standard function module IQS4_ADD_DATA_NOTIFICATION.

I added a custom button on the UI that retrieves data from the selected line and then calls this function module to perform the update. However, based on RAP architecture, function modules or BAPIs that contain explicit COMMIT WORK or ROLLBACK WORK statements are not allowed. When triggering the action, I encounter a BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT dump.

I am aware of possible approaches such as using a background job or bgPF (background processing framework). However, I am looking for guidance on the most suitable and recommended solution for this specific scenario

Has anyone faced a similar issue or found a suitable workaround for this scenario in RAP?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices.

Thank you,

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Collaborative draft
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the singleton pattern is used when generating the draft enabled RAP BO. One attribute of this pattern is that all entities are locked, so it is not possible for multiple users to work with the same configuration at the same time.

This blog describes how to avoid this restriction by using collaborative drafts.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Enable collaborative draft

In the generated behavior definition, add "collaborative" to the draft annotation:

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_COMPANYCODE_S unique;
strict;
with collaborative draft;

Add the draft query annotation and the share action to the singleton entity definition:

define behavior for ZI_CompanyCode_S alias CompanyCodeAll
draft table ZDEMO_CC_D_S query ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
[...]
{
  draft action Share;
  [...]
}

 Right-click the singleton draft table and choose New Data Definition to create the CDS view for the draft query:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Draft Query View'
define view entity ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
  as select from zdemo_cc_d_s
{
  key singletonid                   as Singletonid,
      lastchangedatmax              as Lastchangedatmax,
      transportrequestid            as Transportrequestid,
      draftentitycreationdatetime   as Draftentitycreationdatetime,
      draftentitylastchangedatetime as Draftentitylastchangedatetime,
      draftadministrativedatauuid   as Draftadministrativedatauuid,
      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

You need to enhance the authorization of the involved users. In SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition you assign business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_RAP_DRAFT_PC to the user, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition authorization object S_DRAFT.

User TB is creating a new company code 400 while at the same time user PW is editing company code 100. All changes are recorded on the same transport request.

Since the collaborative draft is enabled for the singleton entity, the save and discard draft action are applied to all changes. In practice, this means that discarding the draft or saving must be coordinated between the users involved.
For completely independent editing of individual objects, you must develop a RAP BO without a singleton pattern.

entry.title: Re: BtchStgLoc - LC when this mrp element will be created...
entry.content:
In MD04 (Stock/Requirements List), every line you see is an MRP element. These elements are created automatically by the system when certain transactions or planning activities occur. The descriptions you saw in table T457T are simply the text labels for those element codes (like DepReq, BStLoc, StLoc, etc.)

Here is how the MRP elements get created:

Dependent Requirements (DepReq) → Generated when a higher‑level BOM component is planned. For example, if a finished good requires a raw material, MRP creates a dependent requirement for that raw material.

Purchase Requisitions (PRqRel, PurRqs) → Created when MRP runs and determines external procurement is needed.

Planned Orders (PlOrd) → Created when MRP decides in‑house production is required.

Stock Transfer Requisitions / Batch Storage Location (BStLoc) → Created when MRP determines stock needs to be moved between plants or storage locations.

Sales Orders (OrdRes) → Appear when a customer order is entered in SD; they consume available stock.

Reservations (ResReq) → Created when a production order or maintenance order reserves material.

Schedule Lines (SchLne) → Created when scheduling agreements are in place.

So, the MRP element you saw in MD04 (for example, BStLoc) was created because MRP determined that material had to be transferred from one storage location or batch to another. The system automatically generates that requirement during the planning run or when a transaction (like a sales order or production order) triggers it.

entry.title: Re: Medical device stock to clinic and traceability for patient billing
entry.content: Hi Kartik, This is a very relevant scenario in the medical device industry, especially where reuse, traceability, and service-based billing are involved. Instead of going with a full consignment model (which can become quite complex to manage across multiple clinics), a better approach is to treat the device as a loaner or equipment-based model. You can send the device using a free-of-charge or loan delivery in SAP SD while maintaining strict serial number tracking, and avoid billing at this stage since revenue is generated from the service, not the product itself. This keeps the process lightweight while still maintaining control and compliance. When the device is returned, you can process it through a return delivery and update its lifecycle status (for example: in use → returned → under processing → available → scrapped). It is highly recommended to integrate this with SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) or serial number management so each device is treated like an equipment asset. This allows you to track usage history, number of cycles, and ensure proper compliance (especially important for FDA-regulated environments). In fact, many manufacturers working on reusable and high-precision medical components—like AK Stamping—focus heavily on engineering accuracy, traceability, and ISO-certified processes to meet strict medical standards. For billing, since the actual revenue comes from the patient data/report, the ideal approach is to create a service order after the device is returned and data is downloaded. This service order should link the device serial number, hospital/clinic, and patient (via a reference or custom field). Billing can then be triggered using standard service billing methods (like resource-related billing or SD service billing). The key here is to maintain a strong mapping between device serial number, patient, and customer, ensuring full traceability and audit readiness. Overall, a hybrid approach using SD for logistics and PM/CS for tracking and service processing works best. It keeps operations simple while ensuring compliance, reuse tracking, and accurate service-based billing.

entry.title: Aprender Jugando: El Poder de la Simulación SAP ERPsim en el Aula > By ERPsim Lab - HEC Montréal
entry.content:
Página de registro: Aprender Jugando: El Poder de la Simulación SAP ERPsim en el Aula Tickets, Tuesday, May 12 from 2 pm to 5 pm CEST | Eventbrite
 
 
Overview
Masterclass SAP ERPsim: Claves de Implementación. Taller en español impartido por la profesora Andrea Ollo, UPNA, España

Descripción del Evento

¿Buscas una forma real de conectar la teoría empresarial con la práctica profesional? Te invitamos a este taller exclusivo diseñado para docentes que desean dar el salto a la gamificación con SAP y ERPsim.

Bajo la guía de la Prof. Andrea Ollo, experta certificada en ERPsim, descubrirás cómo las simulaciones de negocio en tiempo real pueden aumentar drásticamente el compromiso y el aprendizaje de tus estudiantes.

¿Qué aprenderás en estos 90 minutos?

  • El ecosistema ERPsim: Entenderás por qué es la herramienta líder en simulación empresarial SAP a nivel mundial con foco en el ERPsim Manufacutring Game.
  • Dinámica de Juego: Cómo gestionar una empresa virtual en tiempo real .
  • Valor Académico: Estrategias para integrar la simulación en tus guías docentes y evaluar competencias clave.
  • Primeros pasos: Todo lo que necesitas saber para lanzar tu primera simulación con éxito.

Estructura del Programa

Sesión Opcional (Infomación, Introducción): 28 de abril, 14:00 – 15:30 (CET).

  • Sesión dirigida a profesores que no han utilizado simulaciones de negocios o SAP previamente en sus clases con el objetivo de garantizar que todos los participantes comiencen el taller principal con la misma base técnica.

Taller Principal: 12 de mayo de 2026 | 14:00 – 17:00 (CET).

  • Sesión intensiva de 3 horas de inmersión total.

Información Clave

  • Ponente: Prof.  Andrea Ollo (Instructor Certificado ERPsim Nivel 3).
  • Alcance: Dirigido a profesores y académicos de la región Iberia.
  • Requisitos: No es necesaria experiencia previa en SAP.
  • Patrocinio / Con la colaboración de: SAP Academic Partnerships y ERPsim Lab, HEC Montréal

Acerca de Andrea Ollo

Andrea Ollo López (Pamplona, España) es profesora titular en el Departamento de Gestión de Empresas de la Universidad Pública de Navarra (UPNA). Es licenciada en Administración y Dirección de Empresas por la UPNA y doctora en el programa interuniversitario “Doctorate in Economics, Management and Organization (Business Economics)”.

Su actividad docente e investigadora se centra en el área de Organización de Empresas, con especial interés en la gestión de recursos humanos y el papel de las personas como factor clave de competitividad. Ha impartido docencia en grado y máster, y cuenta con una amplia experiencia en la dirección de trabajos académicos y participación en proyectos de investigación nacionales e internacionales.

En el ámbito de la innovación docente, la profesora Andrea está especializada en el uso de simuladores empresariales, particularmente en entornos SAP. Es formadora certificada como Train the Trainer (TTT) de ERPsim, y cuenta con experiencia en la implementación del simulador ERPsim Manufacturing Game.

entry.title: Transaction cannot be revived after delayed abortion(RAP_RUNTIME 019) - Must ROLLBACK After Failures
entry.content:
1. Symptom (ST22 / Dump Keywords)

A typical dump looks like this (same category as in your case):

  • Runtime Error: RAISE_SHORTDUMP
  • Exception: CX_SADL_DUMP_APPL_MODEL_ERROR
  • T100: RAP_RUNTIME / 019
  • Short Text: Transaction cannot be revived after delayed abortion (BO: I_ENTERPRISEPROJECTTP_2)

When debugging you may also notice RAP internal state like:

  • CL_RAP_BHV_PROCESSOR->IF_RAP_LEGACY_TRANSACTION~MV_ABORT = abap_true

Meaning: RAP has already marked the logical transaction as aborted (delayed abortion). It must not be reused.

2. Root Cause: RAP Transaction State + Delayed Abortion

A RAP EML “logical transaction” typically consists of two phases:

  1. Transaction phase
  • MODIFY ENTITIES runs validations/determinations/checks.
  • Errors are usually returned in FAILED / REPORTED
  1. Save phase
  • COMMIT ENTITIES triggers the save sequence (finalize/save, etc.).
  • Errors again come back via FAILED / REPORTED (in the commit response this is the LATE response).

If anything fails in either phase, RAP can enter delayed abortion:
Your ABAP code continues running,but the RAP logical transaction becomes unusable.
Any subsequent EML using that aborted transaction context may lead to RAP_RUNTIME 019.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF <BO> ... FAILED ls_failed_tx REPORTED ls_reported_tx.
COMMIT ENTITIES ... FAILED ls_failed_save REPORTED ls_reported_save.

" If commit failed but you don't rollback here,
" next EML call can dump (RAP_RUNTIME 019)
MODIFY ENTITIES OF <BO> ...

Key point: Never continue with another EML step after a failure without rollback.

5. Correct Example Using I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report zdemo_rap_eml_epp3
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zdemo_rap_eml_epp3.

PARAMETERS:
      p_uuid TYPE sysuuid_c36 OBLIGATORY,
      p_desc TYPE string      DEFAULT 'Demo update from EML'.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  DATA lv_uuid_x16 TYPE sysuuid_x16.

  TRY.
              cl_system_uuid=>convert_uuid_c36_static(
                  EXPORTING
                      uuid     = p_uuid
                  IMPORTING
                      uuid_x16 = lv_uuid_x16 ).
        CATCH cx_uuid_error.
              WRITE: / 'Invalid UUID format. Use 36-char UUID (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx).'.
              RETURN.
  ENDTRY.

  DATA:
        ls_failed_tx     TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED   i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_reported_tx   TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_failed_save   TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED   LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_reported_save TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3.

  TRY.
              " Transaction phase
              MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
                  ENTITY EnterpriseProject
                  UPDATE FIELDS ( ProjectDescription )
                  WITH VALUE #(
                      ( %key-ProjectUUID      = lv_uuid_x16
                          ProjectDescription    = p_desc )
                  )
                  FAILED   ls_failed_tx
                  REPORTED ls_reported_tx.

              IF ls_failed_tx IS NOT INITIAL.
                    WRITE: / 'MODIFY ENTITIES failed (transaction phase) -> ROLLBACK ENTITIES'.
                    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
                    RETURN.
              ENDIF.

              " Save phase
              COMMIT ENTITIES
                  BEGIN RESPONSE OF i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
                  FAILED   ls_failed_save
                  REPORTED ls_reported_save.
              COMMIT ENTITIES END.

              IF ls_failed_save IS NOT INITIAL.
                    WRITE: / 'COMMIT ENTITIES failed (save phase) -> ROLLBACK ENTITIES'.
                    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
                    RETURN.
              ENDIF.

              WRITE: / 'COMMIT ENTITIES OK.'.

        CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
              WRITE: / 'Unexpected exception:', lx_root->get_text( ).
              " Keep the EML contract: if anything goes wrong, end the logical transaction.
              ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
  ENDTRY.

Two important details in this example

  1. COMMIT ENTITIES requires LATE response types
  • RESPONSE FOR FAILED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
  • RESPONSE FOR REPORTED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
  1. “Fail fast” with rollback
  • If FAILED is not initial in either phase, rollback and end the logical transaction.
  • Start the next business step in a new logical transaction context.

 

8. FAQ: Why not ROLLBACK WORK?

In RAP EML, use the EML-compatible statement:

  • Errors in EML logical transaction → ROLLBACK ENTITIES

ROLLBACK WORK is lower-level LUW handling and can lead to semantic mismatches with RAP’s logical transaction handling. The dump you saw is fundamentally about the RAP logical transaction being aborted and still being reused.

entry.title: Re: File content in ASCII and file characterset in EBCDIC
entry.content:
According to the SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity standard behavior, SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity does not perform character set conversion for inbound or outbound files.

SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity acts as a secure transport, routing, and validation layer. Incoming files are technically validated, including file structure and character encoding. If a file is delivered with a character set of EBCDIC, it will be rejected, even if the visible content appears to be ASCII.

There is no configuration option in SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity to:

  • Ignore the character set
  • Automatically convert EBCDIC to ASCII / UTF‑8
  • Remap character encoding during processing

This is intentional and by design, as SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity does not perform file transformations.

Recommended resolution

  • The bank must provide files in a supported character encoding (for example ASCII or UTF‑8), as agreed in the interface definition.
  • If EBCDIC is used on the bank side, the conversion must happen before the file reaches SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity, for example:
    • On the bank side, or
    • Via an external middleware / preprocessing layer outside SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity

Once the file reaches SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity, character set conversion is not supported.

Relevant SAP documentation

  • SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity – Security Capabilities
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/security-capabilities

  • SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity to Bank / FSI
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sap-multi-bank-connectivity-to-bank-fsi

  • SFTP (H2H) Integration – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sftp-h2h

Summary:
SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity cannot ignore or convert EBCDIC character sets. Files must be delivered in a supported encoding; otherwise, they will be rejected.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate
entry.content:
The local configuration in SOAMANAGER is also called automatic configuration. Rather than configured manully, the logical port is configured by the task automatically in the background.

However, in this mode, the authentication doesn't support OAuth2. To get rid of basic auth, an option is to use client certificate.

Configuration Steps

let's take sales order notification outbound as an example.

This is the office help document:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/ed66f965148548a9bb76c24be640d7fc.html?locale=en-US

1. Generate certificate files and upload it into S4 HANA

Please refer to the configuration steps described here 

2. Activate the AIF

TCODE: /AIF/SETUP,  switch off 'test mode'

3. Unpack AIF Content

TCODE: /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

4. Ensure that the Web service Point-to-Point is active in Customizing

5. Download WSDL file from SAP Accelerator Hub

https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.sap.com%2Fapi%2FOP_CO_SDSLS_ESR_SALES_ORDER_NOTIF%2Foverview

6. Modify WSDL

Add namespace Prefix definitions

xmlns:wsp="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2004/09/policy"
xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/oasis-wss-wssecurity-policy-1.2"

Add a new policy before the wsdl:type section

    <wsp:Policy wsu:Id="ClientCertificateBindingPolicy">
        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
            <wsp:All>
                <!-- Mandate mutual authentication using X.509 Certificate -->
                <sp:TransportBinding xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/ws-sx/ws-securitypolicy/200702">
                    <wsp:Policy>
                        <sp:TransportToken>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <sp:HttpsToken>
                                    <wsp:Policy>
                                        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                            <!-- Require Client Certificate -->
                                            <sp:RequireClientCertificate/>
                                        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    </wsp:Policy>
                                </sp:HttpsToken>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:TransportToken>
                        <sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    <sp:Basic256Sha256/>
                                </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                    </wsp:Policy>
                </sp:TransportBinding>
            </wsp:All>
        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
    </wsp:Policy>

add the following policy reference inside wsdl:binding section

        <wsp:Policy>
            <wsp:PolicyReference URI="#ClientCertificateBindingPolicy"/>
        </wsp:Policy>

Update target endpoint

For example, if this is the service endpoint to be consumed:

https://api.example.com:50001/v1/users

 find out below string in the WSDL and replace it

https://host:port/

This is how the WSDL looks like after the modification

7. Create Profile

choose Technical Administration —> Profile

create a name, say Client_Cert

select SSL, then choose client certification

activate the profile

8. Create Provider System

choose Technical Administration  -> Provider Systems.

choose 'create special' , then 'create Third Party System'

input a system name and select the profile defined just now

input the username password for the SAP S4HANA system

DO NOT activate the system. Just save it. 

Open the system in change mode

go to tab WSDL Assignment. choose to upload the WSDL modified.

After uploading the WSDL,  you can save and activate the provider system.

Check connection, you should get this view

9. Maintain 'Logon Data'

click create button

fill in a name which does make sense for future reading

choose User/Password or X.509, select the PSE maintained with .pfx file

activate it.

10. Local Configuration

go to Service Administration  --> Local Integration Scenario Configuration

click save button

Fill up a name which does make sense

Under Service Definitions, add the inbound service SalesOrderBulkRequest_In and assign the profile you created earlier.

Next step, As the service group, choose  SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF

With this service group, you add Sales Order (A2A) and Sales Order - Send Processing Notification (A2A).

For the provider IBC reference, choose the provider system that you created earlier.

select the logon data maintain just now in the next step

confirm and activate the configuration

11. Process Tasks

choose Service Administration ->  Pending Tasks

click process task List

if everything has configured correctly , you should get a view similar to this:

 

12. Check the Logical Port generated

goto ‘Web Service Configuration', search for ‘SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF’

there should be logical port generated with a numberic id

in the security tab, it should be similar to this view

in the transport setting tab, you should see the replacing URL modified to WSDL filled in the URL field

Since the logical port is via local configuration or automatically configured, none of the field is editable. This is a big contrast to the manual configuration. 

Conclusion

1. By using OpenSSL, you should be able to generate the .pfx file which uploaded into SAP S4

2. By modifing WSDL, you should be able to generate the client certificate authentication configured in the logical port.

Reference

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ws-local-configuration..html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/service-group-configuration-in-soamanager-using-ssl-certificates/ba-p/13572482

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth
entry.content:
Basic authentication (Auth) is inherently insecure because it transmits credentials in base64-encoded cleartext with every request, making them vulnerable to interception without HTTPS. It lacks session management, logout functionality, and support for multifactor authentication (MFA), making it vulnerable to replay and brute-force attacks.

Here are Key Disadvantages of Basic Auth:

  • Credential Exposure: Username and password are sent in every request. If not using HTTPS, these are trivially captured and decoded, as Base64 is encoding, not encryption.
  • Replay Attacks: Because credentials are sent repeatedly, a captured header allows an attacker to replay the request and gain access until the password changes.
  • Lack of Session Control: There is no native logout or session expiration mechanism. Once credentials are leaked, they remain valid indefinitely until the password is changed.
  • Poor Security Controls: Basic Auth often lacks support for modern security features, including rate-limiting, account lockout after failed attempts, and multi-factor authentication (MFA).
  • Poor User Experience: Browsers often cache credentials until closed, making it difficult for users to sign out or switch accounts without closing the browser.

While it is easy to implement for internal tools, its lack of security makes it unsuitable for production environments or systems containing sensitive data, especially when the connection is over internet.

Here is my experience how to get rid of basic auth for S/4HANA in difference connection types, without middleware layer. For Inbound/Outbound IDOC connection without PI/CPI as the middleware, a Jco server should be deployed to derive IDOC into an industry standard format, like XML. This design does support client certificate. However since IDOC + Jco is a bit old communication combination, I am not going to address it in a separated postal. Please refer to the web service on client certificate.

The blog post is more for the integration developer rather than the UI developer.

When S/4 HANA works as a service provider(server)

  • API based on OAuth
  • SOAP Inbound web service on the client certificate

When S/4 HANA works as a service consumer(client)

  • RFC Destination on OAuth2
  • RFC Destination on Client Certificate
  • SOAMANAGER Manual Configuration on Oauth2
  • SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate

entry.content:
Description:

Hello Team,

I am currently facing an issue while attempting to generate an intercompany billing request using the app "Generate Intercompany Billing Request" as part of the scope item 4AN – Intercompany Billing for Cross-Company Cost Accounting Postings.

Despite completing all required configuration steps as per the official setup instructions, the system returns an error during the creation of the intercompany billing request.

System Details:

  • System: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Scope Item: 4AN – Intercompany Billing
  • App Used: Generate Intercompany Billing Request
  • Business Scenario: Cross-company cost allocation requiring intercompany billing

Actual Result:

Request:

Attachments:

  • Screenshot of the error message
  • Configuration screenshots (if needed)

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Salesperson in ME21M - Create Purchase Order
entry.content:
The sales person data in PO gets filled from BP supplier purchasing data (LFM1-VERKF). 

We had a aimilar issue with sales person data being populated on PO and we could see that in our S/4 system this field is 'hidden' in the field group settings of vendor account group (which made it invisible in BP) but the migrated data can be seen in LFM1 table. Once the we performed the config changes to make the field optional, the field with value is visible in BP purchasing view and modifiable.

entry.title: Spot Tender in SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean Service based Settlement. (Default Charge Type)
entry.content:
This blog post will help readers, customers, and SAP TM users familiarize themselves with the SAP Transportation Management’s Spot procurement functionality in SAP S/4 HANA 2025 with Lean Service based Transportation Settlement.

SAP Transportation Management’s spot procurement is a subset of the Tendering process having 2 broad categories i.e. Contracted and non-contracted carrier.

In my previous blog post I highlighted step-by-step process for one of the many ways in which we can carry out spot tender in version below S/4 HANA TM 2025. Going forward the process has evolved compared to previous version and also based on the business needs and processes.

Below is the link to PART 1 of Spot Tender which clearing calls out detailed process steps:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-members/spot-tender-in-sap-s-4hana-transportation-management/ba-p/13541617

You can read more about SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management -Tendering in the Application Help for SAP S/4HANA
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/c336c907db3742eeb97e9138dd419970.html?locale=en-US

Note: I have carried out the demonstration with relevant screen shot from an internal system for ease of understanding. There’s no relevance or relation to any external source.

Use Case

Use case is for both the customers in Transportation & Logistics Industry (T&L) as well as shippers from Retail, CPG, manufactures etc. who procure services to move their loads or are in business of providing transportation services and have implemented SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean service procurement.

Assumption: 

The customer is a CPG company and implemented SAP S/4HANA 2025 SAP Transportation Management along with Lean service procurement.

They have yearly contracts with the carriers to move certain capacities on a given corridor/ route. Alternatively, they may also reach out to carriers who don’t have a contract. Contract in the SAP TM world should be referred as FRA (Freight Agreement).

Challenge:

During certain festive seasons the demand on a particular corridor may outweigh the capacity already procured during the start of the year. In this situation, it becomes really very important to meet the increasing demand and ship the goods to our esteemed customers within the stipulated time frame.

Towards the end of the year the procurement team along with Finance would like to analyze the premium paid as transportation services and how it can be curtailed in the upcoming fiscal year. Alternatively, For many years clients have been requesting to book premium cost in a different G/L and replace the std ADJ charge into a more business defined terminology.

In an ideal world, in the absence of the above the cost segregation is cumbersome and involves additional resources and efforts which aren’t quantified. For the leadership as well, this seems to be a non-transparent process with certainly being a  post facto process to adjust the book of records.

Solution:

SAP S/4 HANA TM 2025+ with Lean Service procurement has come out with an out of the box provision to modify the std ADJ charge type during spot tender process which provides step-by-step visibility and more opportunity to drive the tender process both from process as well as financial stand point. (*some limitations and observations shared in the trail blog post)

Scenario:

Let’s move directly at the stage where a FRO is created and subcontracted. Further, we have received a response for an RFQ from the carrier with evaluation completed. We will begin from the step where the user has decided to manually award the carrier.

We start with an assumption that all Tender relevant configs are in place, as the focus is not on setting up the system but on functionality walk-through.

Pictorial representation of the demonstrated functionality:

Basic Tender flow (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Note: Let us now consider two scenarios in the case of Award quotation:

  1. Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
  2. Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Scenario 1: Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
 
Step 1: User is trying to award a carrier and stumbled upon below messages.

The system has hindered the carrier award process and will not allow to proceed further.

Let us analyze how to can overcome the errors.

Step 2: In order to resolve above error, we can be leverage below BADI and implementation as showcased below.

BADI: /SCMTMS/TCC_TEND_DFLT_CHRG_TYP

Here you can observe that I have created a new charge type ZADITYA to replace ADJ. (Charge type created for representation purpose only)

The glaring difference compared to previous blog/version is that system now restricts and aborts the award process which wasn’t the case earlier.

Step 3: Let us try to award the carrier Post BADI implementation.

The popped messages don’t hinder with the carrier award process and carrier is successfully awarded.

Step 4: When we observe the Charges tab, awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and system has assigned the entire spot tender amount i.e. 4500 USD earlier viewed under Adjusted/Quoted Amount charge type to ZADITYA.

You can further map the Charge type to different G/L to apportion the cost accordingly. (* Not part of this blog Post)

Now let us look at the Flip side i.e. Awarding Carrier having an FRA (Freight Agreement)

Scenario 2:  Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Note: The BADI implementation is still active.

Step 5:Post awarding the carrier, since system determined an FRA, it displayed below messages with no visible warnings nor errors.

Now let’s Analyze the Charge Tab.

Step 6: In this case, the awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and has an existing agreement with charges amounting to 2000 USD. System subtracts agreement charges from the submitted charges i.e. giving a clear picture of the individual charges incurred.

The glaring difference to note in contacted scenario is that system didn’t replace the ADJ charge type but continues to work as previous version.

You will have to continue to apportion premium charges as part of ADJ charge type with 2 different ways of handling the process for the customer having both active scenarios.

*Will share more insights but at this stage the above is the observed system behavior.

The demonstration in this blog post doesn’t focus on the configs to setup the system nor does it highlight the further freight settlement process.

Hope this article helps all of us understand the upcoming new functionality of the spot tender process in the SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2025 with Lean service procurement. There may be multiple different ways to carry out the tendering process but, in this demonstration, I chose to keep it generic and simple.

Thank you for taking time and effort to read this.

Since the tests are carried out with limited resources and purely based on observations, any Feedback/comments in any form will be highly appreciated.

SAP Cloud Transport Management SAP Transportation Management Travel and Transportation SAP S/4HANA SAP Business Network for Logistics

entry.title: Re: deactivation of company code
entry.content:
Dear @jerrysam,

There is no supported standard procedure in CBC to delete, deactivate or close an existing active company code. SAP states this directly in Note 3138435 and CBC documentation states that once an organizational unit is confirmed, it cannot be deleted or unassigned in Set Up Organizational Structure. The limited CBC deprecation process applies to storage locations and storage-location-to-warehouse relations, not to company codes. So in Public Cloud the correct approach is not technical deletion of the company code, but retirement from operational use

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3138435

Recommended procedure before retiring an existing company code:

1. In CBC, each country/region must have at least one default company code. So before retiring a company code, confirm that another company code remains available as the default for that country/region where applicable

2. CBC documentation shows that the organizational structure is built starting from the company code and that units such as plants and sales organizations are created under it. Practically, this means those dependent business structures and processes should be moved, stopped or no longer used before you retire the company code

3. SAP documents that after such an organizational entity is created, customers may notice there is no delete option and the CBC guide confirms that confirmed organizational units cannot be deleted or unassigned

4. SAP documents that Manage Posting Periods is the app used to open and close posting periods. This is the first standard control to prevent further postings in that company code

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/98bd1b5825b0a107e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/a11940e4f97143d98a82e4741827e580.html

5. SAP Note 3138435 gives the supported workaround: because an active/productive company code cannot be deleted, deactivated, or closed, postings should be blocked via Manage G/L Account Master Data using the Blocked for Posting control. SAP Help also documents the related Implementation Status / Not Used option in the same app

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/34428754dccbe85ee10000000a44176d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/2052bdb5d8fc4b6d9d787ea991e3613d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/7d98e39c63384590897f8afc80ed54a0.html

6. For wrongly created or unwanted company codes, SAP documented guidance is to keep the company code and mark it with something like DO NOT USE, rather than trying to move or remove it

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3426261

IMPORTANT:
If the request is only about Asset Accounting, SAP provides a separate function call Make Company Code Settings Asset Accounting-Specific. SAP documents that this app can be used to block or deactivate the company code for Asset Accounting and once the status is set to Deactivated, it cannot be changed back. This is not a full CBC/company-code deletion

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/8e71a76adeb84940b785936842cc7574.html

Summary:

The supported approach is to retire the company code from use, not to delete or deactivate it in CBC. The practical sequence is validate CBC dependencies and default company code, stop dependent business use, close posting periods, block G/L postings and keep the company code marked as DO NOT USE

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3580500

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Mastering Inbound Execution in SAP EWM: Integrating WOCR with POSC, LOSC with Door determination
entry.content:
Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMCORP etc. He worked on various type of project flavor like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

1.Abstract:

This paper describes the scenario wherein warehouse is receiving the raw material from Vendor and process flow is as follows:

#1 Enter truck through warehouse door -> Unload the material in staging area ->Put away

#2 Put away is not possible but through Intermediate Bin

#3 Need to group warehouse tasks

Seeing the above requirement, we need to design the Door determination, POSC (process-oriented storage control), LOSC (Layout oriented storage control) and WOCR (warehouse order creation rule). In this blog, I have designed said process, which will help fellow consultants to understand the business requirement and end to end design process.

a) Door Determination: System automatically decides which unloading door, a truck should go to when goods arrive.

b) WOCR (Warehouse order creation rule): This rule, group the warehouse tasks into a warehouse order, which is the efficient way to execute the warehouse order by a warehouse person. This will help to improve the productivity of warehouse person keeping the similar warehouse tasks (area, activity, quantity etc.) in respective warehouse order. This also tells, how many warehouse tasks will be in one warehouse order.

c) POSC  (Process oriented storage control process): This is one of the storage control types, based on process type. This defines; how many steps will be there for Inbound/outbound processes.  Example of steps in inbound/outbound process is as follows:

c1) Inbound : Unloading -> Deconsolidation -> Putaway

c2) Outbound: Picking-> Packing->Staging

d) LOSC (Layout oriented storage control process): This is one of the storage control types, based on path. It defines the route (physical path) goods should take inside warehouse base of designed layout. This is applicable, when direct movement is not possible or warehouse has conveyors or automated warehouses like ASRS/MFS.

WOCR finalize how warehouse task (WT) sorted and grouped into warehouse orders (WO). This applies when WT are created and need to define that how many WT will be under warehouse orders. This will help, what would be total resource workload and how it will be assigned to resources. This improves warehouse efficiency by grouping tasks logically into executable units, reducing travel time and optimizing resource usage. WOCR play a crucial role in optimizing inbound execution by intelligently grouping warehouse tasks into executable units (Warehouse Orders).

WOCR enhances inbound warehouse operations by grouping tasks intelligently, reducing travel time, improving resource utilization, and ensuring efficient execution of put away and handling processes in SAP EWM.

This title, consider a scenario wherein Inbound process needs WOCR along with POSC + LOSC. Normally POSC triggers from Warehouse process type (WPT) and following by LOSC and top of that WOCR will be applied. This use case considers a scenario wherein both storage control processes are required at same time. In this example, POSC will have two processes (Unloading + Put away) and during put way only LOSC will be applied (move material to intermediate bin), top of that WOCR will be applied on all Warehouse order creation.

Here Process oriented storage control is having below two steps:

#1 Unloading

#2 Put away

However, during step #2, Layout oriented storage control triggers (intermediate bin) depending upon the final storage bin and move the material to Intermediate bin and then complete the put away process.

2.0 Introduction:

Here, we are having business scenario for Inbound raw materials. This process will start from Door determination, unloading the material at staging area and finally put away, however direct put away is not possible because of Intermediate bin. Technically process will start from door determination, POSC, LOSC and top of that WOCR will be implemented.

Need to make the needed customization, master data and finally end to end process.

Door determination

System automatically decides which unloading door, a truck should go to when goods arrive. 

Warehouse order creation rule(WOCR)

This control to sort and group the WT task and also finalize that that how many WT will be under one warehouse order

Process oriented storage control(POSC)

This control the logical process steps like Unloading, Deconsolidation, Labelling, Quality inspection, Packing, Unpacking, staging, put away etc.

Layout oriented storage control(LOSC)

This control the physical movement of material inside the warehouse, like staging area, Conveyor routing, Intermediate bins etc.

3.0 Detailed process:

As per the business requirement, Door determination will be made, follows by POSC, which will be triggered from WPT.  Next LOSC will be triggered during put away (step from POSC). WOCR will be applied on all warehouse order. In this paper, using WOCR + POSC + LOSC, for Inbound process.

#1 Configuration of WOCR:

Filter: This is required to identify the weight/volume of warehouse task at item/subtotal level.

FLT1 is created for Item level weight less than 10 Kg.

FLT2 is created for Item level weight is more than 10KG and less than 100Kg.

Limit: This defines majorly how many WT per WO.

LTM1 is created one warehouse task per warehouse order.

LTM1 is created two warehouse task per warehouse order.

WOCR: Rule can be defined majorly by WO creation category, Filter, Limit and packing profile

WOC1 was created with FLT1 and LMT1:

WOC2 was created with FLT2 and LMT1

WOC3 was created with FLT1 and LMT2:

WOC4 was created with FLT2 and LMT2:

Search Sequence: This will help to search the respective Warehouse order, as per the conditions

#2 Door Determination:

Configuration and master data settings are as follows:

Configuration

Made three staging area, because we are assuming there are three doors for in-warding the raw material.

Made below three Door determination groups:

Made three doors (DOR1/DOR2/DOR3) and link with door determination group.

Assigned staging area to Door.

b) Master data

b1) Created below bins as staging area under storage type 9010

 

b2) Created below intermediate bin under intermediate storage type 0082

 

b3) Setting Warehouse process type along with staging area group, bay and warehouse door.

#3 POSC and LOSC:

Configuration

Final storage type (ST03) for raw material

Intermediate Storage type (082): This is HU required Storage type

Warehouse process type: 

Created 3030 for LOSC

Created WPT as WP10 for POSC triggering 

Define Control indicator T8 for WPT # WPT10 determination:

POSC: Below external steps (17PT - Put away & 17UN-Unloading) created

Made POSC as "POS5" and assigned two steps 17UN and 17PT -Automatic created WT 

LOSC configuration:

#4 Testing

#1) Product Master(/SCWM/MAT1): Created material # MAT-1030 with process type determination as T8 and storage group determination as D001(This will determine door as DOR1)

#2 Inbound delivery (VL31N): 180107761 was created

#3 Inbound delivery distributed in EWM(/SCWM/PRDI): 180107761

Step1: Below WT/WO is created(/SCWM/MON)

#1As product master is having process determination as T8, warehouse process WP10 determined.

#2 As per WOCR, respective WOCR # WOC1 was populated.

WOC1 was triggered because line-item level weight was 5 Kg, so filter FLT1 was triggered and it will create one warehouse per one warehouse order as per LMT1. In this case there was only

#3 Product master is having door determination D001, it determined the goods movement bin.

Step2: AS soon as unloading process is completed and destination storage type is ST16, so LOSC triggered and one WO # 1415 was created to move the material to Intermediate bin. And WOC1 was also populated. Next WT # 1862 is under hold.

Step3: As soon as WO # 1415 confirmed, WT # 1862 becomes active.

Step4: After WT # 1862 confirmed material moved to final bin ST16-08

#5 Conclusion

This scenario demonstrates how POSC and LOSC can be effectively combined with WOCR to manage both process-driven steps and physical movement within a unified inbound flow. While WOCR is not always emphasized in standard inbound scenarios, its use adds significant value by optimizing warehouse task grouping and execution efficiency. Integrating WOCR with POSC and LOSC enables a more structured, scalable, and operationally efficient inbound process aligned with real-world warehouse requirements.

In nutshell this blog will help to fellow consultants for simulating below business scenario:

#1 There was more than one door of various sizes, and some materials are huge in volume, so with door determination process, material will get the correct door. 

#2 Business needs two steps (Unload and put away in a sequence) for raw material inbound process then it is achievable with POSC.

#3 Put away is not possible with final bin, then Intermediate bin is required, here LOSC approach is very much required.

#4 Need to segregate the warehouse order based of item weight then can be achieved by WOCR.

Challenges/important points:

  • Most important, HU is very much required to initiate this process (packing is very much required)
  • POSC does not trigger because either bin is not available or Bins are not sorted.
  • LOSC does not trigger, if correct activity area is no updated in LOSC
  • WOCR will not trigger, if correct weight is not maintained at material master level

entry.title: Where-Used List not working in Eclipse (ADT) for S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I’m currently working in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment and have encountered an issue with the Where-Used List functionality in Eclipse (ABAP Development Tools). Specifically, the feature does not seem to return complete results or is not functioning as expected when trying to analyze object dependencies.

For example, when I perform a Where-Used List on CDS view I_PURCHASEREQUISITIONTP, the system only returns a recently created class (ZCL_MF902_01_VN) which I confirmed is using this CDS view. However, it does not return older existing classes such as ZCJ_MF915_01, even though they are also consuming the same CDS view.

I understand that SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud has certain restrictions compared to on-premise systems, but I would like to confirm whether this behavior is by design, or if there are specific configurations, indexing steps, or authorizations required to make the Where-Used List return complete and accurate results.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or found an alternative approach for dependency analysis in Public Cloud?

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Appikatla Venkata Murali Krishna
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Appikatla Venkata Murali krishna, and I am currently an aspiring SAP MM Consultant. I have completed my studies and have been actively building my skills in SAP S/4HANA with a focus on Materials Management (MM).

I have worked on real-time scenarios like procurement processes, freight vendor integration, and inventory management, which helped me understand how businesses manage materials efficiently using SAP. I am passionate about learning new SAP technologies and improving my functional knowledge to grow in this field.

As part of the Hello SAP Community Initiative, I’m excited to share my experience. You can read more about the initiative in Hello SAP Community Initiative , which gives the full background and context.

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a successful SAP MM Consultant working on global implementation projects. I aim to contribute to organizations by optimizing procurement processes and improving supply chain efficiency using SAP solutions.

What do you imagine is the future of your topic (SAP, AI, Cloud)?

I believe the future of SAP lies in AI and Cloud integration. SAP S/4HANA Cloud combined with AI will automate procurement, predict demand, and optimize supply chains. As an SAP MM professional, I am excited to be part of this transformation.

What is your hobby?

My hobbies include learning new technologies, exploring SAP scenarios, and improving my technical knowledge. I also enjoy watching tech-related content and staying updated with industry trends.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

One of the most interesting projects I worked on was Freight Vendor Integration in SAP MM. In this project, I learned how to configure condition types like FRB1, manage multiple vendors (material and freight), and handle invoice verification. It was fun because it connected real-world logistics with SAP configuration.

What do you enjoy most in your studies/work and why?

What I enjoy most is understanding real-time business processes and configuring them in SAP. It is exciting to see how a business requirement is translated into a system solution. Especially in SAP MM, I enjoy working on purchase orders, inventory, and vendor management.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

This year, I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2025, which was an amazing learning experience for me. It was my first time participating in such a large SAP event, and it gave me exposure to the latest innovations in the SAP ecosystem.

During the event, I attended multiple sessions related to SAP S/4HANA, AI integration, and cloud technologies. I also explored how SAP is transforming business processes using intelligent technologies.

One of the best parts of TechEd was the opportunity to connect with SAP experts, professionals, and fellow learners. It helped me understand real-world industry expectations and motivated me to improve my skills further.

Overall, SAP TechEd was an inspiring experience that strengthened my interest in SAP and gave me clarity about my career path.

And now let’s hear from you!

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

I would love to hear your thoughts and experiences please share them in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Over-valuation of Inventory
entry.content: If you want the invoice difference to reduce inventory rather than hit gain/loss, set the material to moving average price control. Also check and refine the UoM conversion factors to minimize rounding differences. With standard price control, the current posting (to 52531000) is correct by design and cannot be changed at invoice stage in Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: BC_EXT_APPJOB_MANAGEMENT and parameter JobParameterVa...
entry.content:
Hi, duckys57.
You need to provide your parameters as an XML string.  For example, if a job have parameter which is called P_NUM, your xml looks like below:
'<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?><asx:abap xmlns:asx="http://www.sap.com/abapxml" version="1.0"><asx:values><VALUES><item><NAME>P_NUM</NAME><T_VALUE><item><SIGN>I</SIGN><OPTION>EQ</OPTION><LOW>100</LOW><HIGH/></item></T_VALUE></item></VALUES></asx:values></asx:abap>' 
Here you can see that the value of 100 was provided for the parameter P_NUM.  Don't forget to enclose the xml in apostrophes '  '.
You can also create that XML with standard ABAP transformation as below:

METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

DATA lt_parameters TYPE cl_apj_rt_api=>tt_job_parameter_value.
DATA ld_xml TYPE string.

lt_parameters = VALUE cl_apj_rt_api=>tt_job_parameter_value(
( name = 'P_NUM' t_value = VALUE #( ( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = '100' ) ) )
) .
CALL TRANSFORMATION id
SOURCE values = lt_parameters
RESULT XML ld_xml.

out->write( ld_xml ).

ENDMETHOD.

Eventually, you need to provide the XML as a value of JobParameterValues parameter when you call the API, like below:
https://{host}/sap/opu/odata/sap/BC_EXT_APPJOB_MANAGEMENT;v=0002/JobSchedule?JobText='Triggered from outside'&JobTemplateName='Some_Template'&JobUser='Your user'&JobParameterValues='<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?><asx:abap xmlns:asx="http://www.sap.com/abapxml" version="1.0"><asx:values><VALUES><item><NAME>P_NUM</NAME><T_VALUE><item><SIGN>I</SIGN><OPTION>EQ</OPTION><LOW>100</LOW><HIGH/></item></T_VALUE></item></VALUES></asx:values></asx:abap>'
Kind regards,
Vadym

entry.title: Re: MI31 are creating documents with counted status.
entry.content:
If you run MI31 with the option “Set Count Indicator,” the system flags the items as already counted. MI31 is often used for mass creation of inventory documents, and depending on the variant, it can pre‑set the “counted” status.

To fix this, when creating inventory documents, ensure the “Count indicator” is not set. This way, the documents remain in not counted status and can be processed in MI04. If you want more control, create inventory documents with MI01 or adjust them with MI02, ensuring the count indicator is blank.
